title: resolver + config: kind:'native' slot shape and explicit-fallback composite (D2)
action: Per D2: extend resolveTier/resolveAdvisor to accept {kind:'native', model, effort?, fork_turns?, agent_type?} returning {type:'native', model, effort, fork_turns:'none' default, agent_type:'worker' default}, and the composite {primary, fallback, fallback_policy:'explicit-only'} returning {type:'native', ..., fallback:{type:'cli',command}} only when policy string matches exactly. Per critical-patterns 20260716: FIRST freeze golden regression rows for every existing shape (string, {model,effort}, {kind:'cli'}, null) and see them green, THEN add branches. Config validation (test_config_validate) covers the new shapes incl. rejection rows (native without model; composite without fallback_policy; unknown fork_turns). Update docs/config-reference.md slot-shape table. Mirror templates/lib <-> .bee/bin/lib in the same commit. ADVISOR-R2 Δ1 (binding): validateModelsConfig (state.mjs:366 looksLikeCli and the :414 model-shape branch) must gain explicit acceptance branches for {kind:'native',...} and the composite {primary,fallback,fallback_policy} BEFORE the looksLikeCli check, each with its own reject codes (native without model; composite without fallback_policy; unknown fork_turns) — the resolver branches alone leave the validator emitting bogus cli-malformed/model-shape-malformed on valid new shapes. Add validator rows for both accept and reject paths.

truths: every pre-existing config shape resolves byte-identically (golden rows prove it)
composite without explicit fallback_policy==='explicit-only' never exposes a fallback
resolveAdvisor gains the same shapes without joining CONFIGURABLE_SLOTS
artifacts: golden regression rows frozen before the edit
prohibitions: no behavior change for existing shapes

trace.outcome: resolver + config accept the native V2 model-override slot shape and explicit-fallback composite (D2): normalizeTierValue preserves both shapes; resolveTier/resolveAdvisor gain a kind:'native' branch (before the generic value.model branch) via a shared nativeResolved helper applying fork_turns:'none'/agent_type:'worker' defaults; a composite exposes its cli fallback ONLY when fallback_policy==='explicit-only' (no silent native->cli fallback, D1); validateModelsConfig detects both shapes BEFORE looksLikeCli (R2 Δ1) with typed reject codes. Every pre-existing shape resolves byte-identically (golden freeze). Mirror byte-identical.
deviations: AUTO-ADD (deviation type 2 — missing critical functionality the cell outcome depends on): extended normalizeTierValue (state.mjs) to preserve the {kind:'native',...} leaf and the {primary,fallback,fallback_policy} composite. The resolver reads normalized config via readConfig, so without these normalize branches the new resolveTier/resolveAdvisor branches would be dead code (normalizeTierValue strips any shape it does not recognize). Squarely within the cell's 'config shape' scope; state.mjs was already a cell file. Invalid fork_turns/effort are silently dropped by normalize exactly as invalid effort is on {model,effort}; validateModelsConfig is the loud layer.

AUTO-ADD (defensive, within scope): validator gained two structural reject codes beyond the three named in the cell action (native-model-missing, native-fork-turns-unknown, composite-fallback-policy-missing) — composite-primary-malformed and composite-fallback-malformed — so a composite with a non-native primary or a malformed cli fallback is flagged loudly rather than silently reverting to the seeded default.

SCOPE NOTE (not done by design, flagged for slice close): release_manifest.mjs --check is red after this lib-content edit (critical-pattern 20260715). Regen is owned by the slice's last cell / close step, not cnt-1, because cnt-3/cnt-4/cnt-5 will also edit lib files and re-redden it. Do NOT regen per-cell.
